blocks.body: A three-year degree focused on software and computer applications — programming, databases, web and the basics of IT. Lighter on heavy maths than B.Tech CSE, it's an affordable, practical route into software, often followed by an MCA.

blocks.title: How you get in
blocks.body: Mostly 10+2 merit; some colleges use an entrance test (e.g. IPU CET).

blocks.title: At a glance
items: A software-first path that doesn't need JEE.
Many employers treat BCA + MCA as comparable to a CS degree for software roles.
Build projects and internships early — they matter more than the tag.
